title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Maplewood, NJ | $900-$2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Maplewood, NJ. Safe, humane bat extraction and prevention. Licensed experts. Call for same-day service!
intro: Maplewood, New Jersey homeowners facing bat infestations need swift, professional intervention to protect their families and property. Our bat removal and exclusion services provide humane, effective solutions that comply with all New Jersey wildlife regulations. We specialize in complete bat extraction, cleanup, and permanent exclusion to keep your Maplewood home bat-free.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of experience serving Maplewood and surrounding communities with specialized bat removal expertise. Our licensed technicians use proven exclusion methods and offer comprehensive warranties, ensuring your home remains protected long after our initial service.
localContext: Maplewood's tree-lined streets and charming older homes—many dating back to the early 1900s—often feature the kind of architectural gaps and attic spaces that attract bat colonies. From properties near Memorial Park to homes in the South Mountain Reservation area, we're familiar with the unique structural challenges that Maplewood residences present for bat prevention.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Maplewood, NJ?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Maplewood typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the colony size, entry points, and the extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before any work begins.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done during specific times of year. New Jersey law prohibits bat exclusion from May 1 to August 15 during maternity season when flightless young are present. Our team follows all state regulations to ensure humane, legal bat removal.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Maplewood attic?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, visible droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, greasy brown stains around gaps, and the distinctive musky odor of bat urine. If you notice any of these signs, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: How do you prevent bats from returning?
faqs.answer: Our exclusion process seals all potential entry points—typically gaps as small as 3/8 inch—using durable materials like steel mesh, caulking, and foam. We install one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but prevent re-entry, then permanently seal those areas once all bats have departed.
